verdicts.note: Score direction survives (therapeutic/treatment-court advocacy is the rubric's sanctioned lower-court proxy for reform-openness), but the confidence does not. There is no interpretive-method evidence anywhere in her dossier — her stated commitments are fairness/equal-justice boilerplate — and the single treatment-programs plank is already the load-bearing evidence for her safety score. Inferring interpretive philosophy from a docket-programming preference is an indirect inference; the guide assigns that low, which also removes the double-counted plank from alignment math on this axis.

review_notes: The most conservative file in the judicial set, and mostly correctly so. Trasen safety +1/high is grounded in a 26-year defense/appellate formation plus an explicit treatment-to-reduce-recidivism platform (record + statement = high). Tse safety -1/medium is the symmetric call: 16-year prosecution formation including homicide trials, honestly capped at medium with no bail/diversion statements documented. The omissions are the right ones: Schaer gets no safety score (AGO civil/appellate formation with only 'earlier public defense' is genuinely uninformative) and neither Schaer nor Tse gets a judicial score from their fairness/readiness boilerplate. Experience scores at -1 for all three fairly reflect a race where every candidate runs a record case and none runs as an outsider; nothing distinguishes them enough to justify different magnitudes. No score leans on the King County Democrats endorsement or the LD endorsement split.
